main.name: Gianluca Donato
main.role: Web Developer & Designer — Buenos Aires, Argentina.

about.title: About
about.body: Web developer and designer working at the intersection of engineering and design. I build landing pages, websites, and web apps using modern tools — Next.js, Tailwind, Supabase — and integrate AI into the process to deliver faster without cutting corners. I stay close to the people I work with: clear communication, honest timelines, and updates throughout the entire process. Always exploring new tools and better ways to build things.

items.name: Stock Manager 📦
items.description: Inventory management and analytics panel for an ecommerce business operating on MercadoLibre. Centralizes listings, distributor links, stock control, and barcode scanning.
items.story:
I work in an e-commerce business that operates through MercadoLibre, actively involved in day-to-day operations and optimizing internal processes through digital solutions. I collaborate in key areas such as inventory control, product analysis, and operational organization.

I developed an internal web application for product and stock management that centralizes critical business information. The tool allows managing MercadoLibre listings, accessing distributor links, handling stock from a proprietary database, and registering products via scanning — significantly reducing manual tasks and improving inventory control.

items.name: Puffa Ecommerce 🛒
items.description: Ecommerce with an unconventional catalog, Mercado Pago checkout, and admin for orders and products. Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind, TypeScript.
items.story:
Puffa is an ecommerce that doesn’t look like the usual store but does the same job: the catalog is presented in a different way while offering full functionality. The idea was to build a fully integrated, functional online store from the ground up.

It includes Mercado Pago integration for payments and an admin panel with an order manager and a product manager. Built with Next.js, Supabase, Tailwind CSS, and TypeScript.

items.name: Catalogy 🧾
items.description: Simple web catalog for small brands or people who want to sell without unnecessary complexity. Built with Next.js and Supabase.
items.story:
I built Catalogy because my mom told me she wanted something simple to show all the cakes and homemade products she cooks and sells, without going through complicated steps.

From that need, I decided to build this app to make catalog creation easier and help her share products in a simple and fast way.

items.name: Mi Inventario 👕
items.description: App to showcase clothing, accessories, perfumes, and more, while discovering new items by browsing other users' inventories.
items.story:
I built Mi Inventario so people and small brands can display what they sell on a page without unnecessary complexity, especially products like clothing, accessories, perfumes, and related items.

Each person has their own inventory, and users can browse other inventories to discover new products. Built with Next.js and Supabase.

items.name: Giannluca's Boiler 🎧
items.description: My own 24/7 boiler/radio where people can listen to my sets in loop or pick a specific one.
items.story:
Since being a DJ is one of my hobbies, I built my own 24/7 boiler/radio to share my sets in a simple and direct way.

People can keep a set playing in loop or choose exactly which one they want to hear at any moment. Built with Next.js.

footer.thanks: Thank you for taking the time to read this. 🖤
